Russian State University of Design and A: Russian State University of Design and Applied Arts (Stroganov University)
MainAboutText: Hi! I'm Eva Varnakova (Ева Варнакова), currently studying at Stroganov University (2020-2026), where I'm focusing on developing my skills and knowledge in art and design. This period has been both challenging and rewarding, as I explore various creative disciplines and techniques that will help me build a solid foundation for my future career. Through my studies, I am not only enhancing my technical abilities but also learning to express my ideas and inspirations through different media.
EtchingStalkerText: This etching, created using the "drypoint" technique, reflects my desire to convey an atmosphere of mystery and melancholy. In the foreground, I depicted a solitary figure standing in a field, surrounded by tall grass and fencing. The silhouette of the person is barely discernible against the twilight sky, which, in my view, evokes a sense of abandonment and loneliness. The drypoint method allowed me to achieve soft lines and deep shadows, adding texture and emotional richness to the work. The rough, scratchy strokes characteristic of this technique convey the roughness of the grass and branches, creating a sense of a damp and cool evening. The contrast between the light sky and the dark, almost black figures and elements in the foreground enhances the drama of the scene. This image is meant to evoke strong emotions in the viewer, leaving room for interpretation and reflection. It invites one to ponder the human condition in the context of nature and time. I aimed to demonstrate my mastery of the drypoint technique, conveying mood and creating a powerful visual image that lingers in the memory for a long time.
EtchingGirlInForestText: This etching depicts a naked, faceless girl sitting on the ground in a dense and mysterious forest. Her pose expresses vulnerability and loneliness; she is curled up, hugging her knees and bowing her head. Long, dark hair cascades over her shoulders and back, enhancing the sense of melancholy and enigma. The forest around her is a complex tangle of branches, roots, and tree trunks, creating a dense, almost impenetrable curtain. Detailed lines and shadows add depth and texture, making the forest appear alive and slightly menacing. The light falling on the girl highlights her against the dark surroundings, creating contrast and drawing the viewer's eye to her figure. Naked and devoid of facial features, the girl becomes a symbol of purity and mystery. Her presence in this wild, almost magical place raises many questions: who is she, why is she here, and what story lies behind this image? This etching, executed in the technique of "etched line", captivates with its atmosphere and evokes a sense of deep contemplation and fantasy in the viewer, inviting them to immerse themselves in a world full of mysteries and untold stories.
EtchingMortuaryParisText: This etching is my rendition, using the "etched line" technique, of a work originally created by Charles Meryon. Meryon was known for his intricate and atmospheric depictions of urban scenes, particularly those of Paris. In this etching, I've aimed to capture the essence of Meryon's detailed and moody cityscape. The scene portrays a dense array of buildings, their facades rich with architectural details. The interplay of light and shadow highlights the texture of the structures, from the rooftops to the intricate windows and chimneys, giving a sense of depth and realism. orner of the detailed urban landscape. By replicating Meryon's style, I aimed to honor his mastery while also immersing myself in the challenges and nuances of the etched line technique. This work reflects my appreciation for Meryon's artistic vision and my dedication to capturing the intricate beauty of urban life as he did.
EtchingShavingsText: This aquatint was created as part of my work at the Stroganov Moscow State Academy of Arts and Industry. In this piece, I sought to capture a sense of eerie mystery and quiet drama through the interplay of light and shadow. The composition features a dimly lit staircase, with intricate railings that cast long, dark shadows across the scene. The surrounding architecture is rendered with detailed attention, showing large windows and walls that seem to recede into darkness. The use of aquatint allowed me to achieve rich, velvety blacks and a wide range of grey tones, enhancing the depth and atmosphere of the piece. In the foreground, a white cat is depicted mid-leap, its form stark against the dark background. The cat adds an element of life and movement to the otherwise still and somber environment, and its presence injects a subtle sense of narrative intrigue. What might this cat be chasing or escaping from? This question invites the viewer to ponder the unseen elements of the story. The technique of aquatint, known for its ability to create smooth gradations of tone, was instrumental in conveying the moody, nocturnal ambiance I aimed for. By carefully controlling the acid etching process, I was able to achieve the soft, atmospheric effects that characterize this piece. Creating this work for the Stroganov Academy allowed me to explore and refine my skills in aquatint, while also expressing a personal vision of mystery and quiet tension. I hope this piece evokes a sense of curiosity and contemplation in those who view it.
BambiDescription: I created illustrations for the book "Bambi" by Felix Salten, using acrylic and tempera techniques. My goal was to convey the magical atmosphere of the forest and its inhabitants, immersing readers in a fairy-tale world.
BambiDescriptionSecondBlock: Working with acrylic allowed me to achieve bright, saturated colors and clear contours. Thanks to the quick drying of acrylic, I could apply multiple layers, creating depth and richness of color in each illustration.
BaggageDescription: When I decided to illustrate Marshak's "Baggage", I knew I wanted to convey its through the play of colors. I chose yellow and orange - bright and cheerful shades that embodied the warmth and energy of the story's words. Starting my work, I began creating a base of yellow tempera. This color filled the pages of my illustrations with light and warmth, as if each one came alive under the morning sun's rays. Yellow became the background on which I began to embody the images of characters and events.
BaggageDescriptionSecondBlock: To add depth and dynamics, I added orange shadows, applied with a pencil. These shadows gave my illustrations volume and liveliness. They seemed to underline every line and shape, making the pictures even more expressive.
BlackChikenDescription: For my illustration of the black hen, I wanted to capture its mysterious and captivating essence. I chose to work with black ink to emphasize the darkness of its feathers and the enigmatic aura surrounding it. Using delicate strokes, I carefully depicted the intricate details of its plumage.
BlackChikenDescriptionSecondBlock: As I worked on the illustration, I found myself captivated by the juxtaposition of the black ink and the bold red accents, each element complementing the other to create a visually arresting depiction of the black hen.
GraphicsNewYork: In 2022, while completing an assignment for the Stroganov University, I created a series of easel graphic works. The task required me to copy one painting by a selected artist and then complement it with three of my own works in a similar style. In my series, I chose an urban theme, inspired by architectural landscapes. In each piece, I focused on conveying the geometry of the cityscape, playing with building lines, shadows, and light. I emphasized sharp vertical and diagonal forms, creating a sense of scale and grandeur in the metropolis.
GraphicsStalker: In 2023, I created a series of works inspired by the film Stalker by director Andrei Tarkovsky for an assignment at Stroganov University. The works were done using watercolor and colored pencils, which allowed me to convey the softness and atmospheric depth of the natural landscape, a key element of the film. In each piece, I aimed to capture the mystical and philosophical atmosphere of the "Zone", where nature feels alive yet mysterious and incomprehensible. Forest scenes, blurred tree contours, muted colors, and dark silhouettes evoke a sense of eerie silence.
GraphicsCity: In 2021, I created a series of graphic works using ink and ballpoint pen for an assignment at Stroganov University, where I had to depict the architecture of Russian cities. In my works, I aimed to convey the bleakness and isolation of old urban neighborhoods, focusing on the textures of facades, balconies, and wires.I used sharp lines and deep shadows to emphasize the decay and abandonment of these places. The ballpoint pen allowed me to create precise strokes, adding texture and structure, while the black-and-white palette enhanced the melancholic atmosphere of the architectural scenes.
